51: **Switzerland** and **Canada**, both level on 4 points at the top of **Group B** with positions not yet separated, meet in a straight shootout for first place — the winner almost certainly locks up the group, while the loser drops into the anxious wait for the **Bosnia and Herzegovina**–**Qatar** result. Both sides arrive unbeaten and full of confidence, making this the most evenly matched and high-stakes opener of the entire matchday.
52: **Bosnia and Herzegovina**, level on 1 point in **Group B** with **Qatar** and positions not yet separated, face **Qatar** — equally unresolved on 1 point — in a match that is effectively a knockout-round play-in for the group's third-place slot. With the top two already pulling clear, both sides know that anything less than a win here likely ends their tournament, guaranteeing an open, attack-minded contest from the first whistle.
49: **Scotland**, sitting 3rd in **Group C** on 3 points, face the sternest test of their campaign against **Brazil**, who are level on 4 points in the group alongside **Morocco** with positions not yet separated. **Scotland** need a result to stay in contention, but **Brazil** arrive needing a win themselves to edge clear at the summit — expect an intense, tactical battle where **Scotland**'s defensive organisation will be severely tested.
50: **Morocco**, level on 4 points in **Group C** with **Brazil** and positions not yet separated, face **Haiti**, rooted to the bottom of the group on zero points after two defeats. **Morocco** will be eager to confirm their place in the knockouts with a convincing victory, though **Haiti** have shown fight throughout the group stage — the margin of this win could yet prove decisive in the final **Group C** standings.
53: **Czechia**, level on 1 point in **Group A** with **South Africa** and positions not yet separated, must overcome group leaders **Mexico**, who sit top of **Group A** on a commanding 6 points. **Mexico** are already through, but a positive result protects their first-place finish and momentum heading into the knockouts — for **Czechia**, only a win keeps their slim hopes of progression mathematically alive.
54: **South Africa**, level on 1 point in **Group A** with **Czechia** and positions not yet separated, face **Korea Republic**, who sit 2nd in the group on 3 points. **Korea** need at minimum a draw to secure their place in the knockouts, but **South Africa** are fully aware that only a win — combined with the right result elsewhere — gives them any realistic path through, ensuring this game carries desperation and intensity in equal measure.
47: **Portugal**, level on 1 point in **Group K** alongside **Congo DR** with positions not yet separated, open the day against **Uzbekistan**, who sit 4th in the group on zero points after their opening defeat. This is a match **Portugal** must win — and win well — to keep pace at the top of the table; **Uzbekistan** will be organised and physical, but the gulf in individual quality should tell if Ronaldo and company bring the urgency this moment demands.
45: **England** and **Ghana** go head-to-head in the standout game of the day, both level on 3 points at the top of **Group L** with positions not yet separated after one round each. The winner seizes clear control at the summit with a matchday to spare — **England** carry the edge in depth and European pedigree, but **Ghana** arrive with momentum and plenty to prove on this stage, making for a genuinely open and high-intensity contest.
46: **Panama**, level on zero points in **Group L** with **Croatia** and positions not yet separated, face **Croatia**, equally unresolved at the bottom on zero points — making this a match neither side can afford to lose. With **England** and **Ghana** already ahead of both on 3 points, a draw here would likely prove fatal to both teams' knockout hopes, so expect urgency and directness from two sides whose tournaments are genuinely on the line.
48: **Colombia**, top of **Group K** on 3 points, face a stiff test from **Congo DR**, who are level on 1 point in the group alongside **Portugal** with positions not yet separated. **Colombia** will be eager to extend their lead at the summit, but **Congo DR** arrive unbeaten and backed by the confidence of holding their ground on Matchday 1 — a point or better here would keep them firmly in contention heading into the final round.
